en_definition: a period of time that is spent doing a particular activity
the normal order and way in which you regularly do things
to move, or move something, from one position or place to another
a change in position or direction
continuing all the time
zh_definition: 持续不断的；连绵不绝的

en_definition: to have developed from something and be strongly influenced by it
the part of a plant that grows under the ground and absorbs water and minerals
a particular set or system of beliefs resulting from the search for knowledge about life and the universe
a set of beliefs or an attitude to life that guides somebody's behaviour
zh_definition: 人生哲学；生活的信条（或态度）

en_definition: a sign, number, letter, etc. that has a fixed meaning, especially in science, mathematics and music
a person, an object, an event, etc. that represents a more general quality or situation
(pl. crises) a time when a problem, a bad situation or an illness is at its worst point
shy, awkward or ashamed, especially in a social situation
to make somebody feel less tired or less hot
the eagerness and willingness to do something without needing to be told or forced to do it
to succeed in getting something, usually after a lot of effort
to direct money, feelings, ideas, etc. towards a particular thing or purpose
a television station
to make a determined effort to deal with a difficult problem or situation
zh_definition: 应付，处理，解决（难题或局面）

en_definition: a hot bright stream of burning gas that comes from something that is on fire
very strong and powerful
the importance of something, especially when this has an effect on what happens in the future
not showing or accepting things as they are
one part of a story that is broadcast on television or radio in several parts
zh_definition: （电视连续剧或广播剧的）一集

en_definition: a result of something that has happened
a change from one opinion or situation to another; the amount by which something changes
if a party, etc. is swinging, there are a lot of people there having a good time
a feeling of not suffering or worrying so much; a feeling of being less unhappy
to make a subject more difficult to understand
to make somebody unable to think clearly or understand something
a person who writes novels
emotionally disturbed (unhappy, disappointed or annoyed)
zh_definition: 不快的；心烦的；不适的（用来形容对某人 / 某事感到难过、失望、生气等情绪）

en_definition: the dark shape that somebody / something's form makes on a surface
a type of job that needs special training or skill, especially one that needs a high level of education
(underwent, undergone) to experience something, especially a change or something unpleasant
used in or connected with surgery
to improve or increase something
to become or make something less unpleasant, painful or severe, etc.
lack of difficulty
to do things in a particular way
to take part in something
to succeed in attracting and keeping somebody's attention and interest
based on your own sense of what is right and fair, not on legal rights or duties
drinks such as beer, wine, etc. that can make people drunk
the use of something in a way that is wrong or harmful
an agreement in which you pay a company money and they pay your costs if you have an accident, injury, etc.
(an amount of) money paid to the government that is based on your income or the cost of goods or services you have bought
a small paper tube that people smoke
to become less, or to make something become smaller in size, number, etc.
very large in size, amount, or number
the ability to spend a lot of time doing something difficult that needs a lot of attention and effort
the desire or determination to be successful, rich, powerful, etc.
(of memories, a description, etc.) producing very clear pictures in your mind
a dream that is very frightening or unpleasant
a story, especially one that might be invented or difficult to believe
to refuse to accept or consider something
a book, that is part of a series of books
the correct relationship in size, degree, importance, etc. between one thing and another or between the parts of a whole
one of many tissues in the body that can tighten and relax to produce movement
of or like a pearl
to make the skin on your face form into lines or folds
lines on your face and skin that you get when you are old
to walk up and down in a small area many times, especially because you are feeling nervous or angry
the speed at which somebody / something walks, runs or moves
the flat part of the face, above the eyes and below the hair
a piece of material, especially cloth, that is hung across a window or opening to make a room or part of a room dark or private
either of the two bones at the bottom of the face that contain the teeth and move when you talk or eat
(pl.) the mouth and teeth of a person or an animal
the soft part of your face that is below your eyes and between your mouth and ears
very clear; perfect
to deal successfully with something difficult
to take something, especially something necessary or pleasant away from someone
unsuccessful; of no value
